# onelogin_user_mapping (Resource)

Manages a OneLogin User Mapping rule.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "onelogin_user_mapping" "example" {
  name    = "Auto-assign Engineering Role"
  match   = "all"
  enabled = true

  conditions {
    source   = "email"
    operator = "contains"
    value    = "@engineering.example.com"
  }

  actions {
    action = "add_role"
    value  = [tostring(onelogin_role.engineering.id)]
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `match` (String) Indicates how conditions are matched. Valid values: "all", "any".
- `name` (String) The name of the user mapping.

### Optional

- `actions` (Block List) A list of actions to perform when conditions are met. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--actions))
- `conditions` (Block List) A list of conditions that must be met for the mapping to apply. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--conditions))
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the user mapping is enabled. Defaults to false.
- `position` (Number) The position of the user mapping in the evaluation order.

### Read-Only

- `id` (Number) The unique identifier of the user mapping.

<a id="nestedblock--actions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `actions`

Required:

- `action` (String) The action type (e.g., "set_role").
- `value` (List of String) The values for the action.


<a id="nestedblock--conditions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `conditions`

Required:

- `operator` (String) The comparison operator (e.g., "contains", "=").
- `source` (String) The source field to evaluate (e.g., "email").
- `value` (String) The value to compare against.
